zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard John Deane de Farley於1559年3月29日出生在英國素瑞阿爾布瑞時，他的父親Richard Farley27歲，母親Jane Arrow24歲。他於1590年11月8日與Elizabetha Aldriche在英國素瑞阿爾布瑞結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1621年4月21日在英國素瑞法爾 格林享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard John Deane de Farley于1559年3月29日出生在英国素瑞阿爾布瑞时，他的父亲Richard Farley27岁，母亲Jane Arrow24岁。他于1590年11月8日与Elizabetha Aldriche在英国素瑞阿爾布瑞结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1621年4月21日在英国素瑞法爾 格林享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Deane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Deagánaigh ‘son of the deacon’ (see Deacon ). In Tipperary it can be from Gaelic Ó Déaghain ‘descendant of the deacon’. In some other cases the surname is of English origin (see Dean ).
English: variant of   Dean  .

variants.name: Digney
meanings: Scottish, Irish, and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either of two minor places in France called Digny (in Eure-et-Loir and Loiret).
Irish: from Gaelic Mac an Deaghánaigh ‘son of the deacon’ (see Deane ).

variants.name: Deacon
meanings: English: from Middle English deken, dicoun ‘deacon, a priest's assistant at church services’ (Old French diacne, Old English diacon, dēacon, both ultimately from Late Latin diaconus, from Greek diakonos ‘servant’). Since deacons were in holy orders they would not be permitted to marry (with allowance for those few already married when ordained), so the surname is probably in most cases a secular nickname rather than the name of a church official, perhaps denoting a deacon's servant.
English: variant of   Dicken  .

Irish (Wexford): of English origin (see 1 and 2 above), taken to Ireland in the 17th century. When the Irish surname is not of English origin, it may be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deocáin ‘descendant of Deocán’, a personal name of uncertain etymology.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Farley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farley, of which there are examples in Berkshire, Derbyshire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Gloucestershire, Staffordshire, Surrey, Wiltshire, Shropshire, and Sussex. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farleigh , Fairley , Fairlie .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghaile (see Farrelly ).

variants.name: Farleigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farleigh, of which there are examples in Hampshire, Devon, Kent, Somerset, Surrey, and Wiltshire. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farley , Fairley , Fairlie .

variants.name: Farlow
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + hlāw ‘hill, tumulus’. This surname may have become confused with Farley .

variants.name: Fearnley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from any of various places called with Old English fearn ‘fern, bracken’ + lēah ‘wood, clearing’, notably the Yorkshire places Farnley near Otley, Farnley near Armley, Farnley Tyas, Fearnley in Wyke, Fernley Hill in Altofts, and perhaps also Fernilee in Derbyshire.
English: variant of   Farley  .

variants.name: Farlee
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Farley  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Richard John Deane de Farley was born on 29 March 1559, in Albury, Surrey, England, his father, Richard Farley, was 27 and his mother, Jane Arrow, was 24. He married Elizabetha Aldriche on 8 November 1590, in Albury, Surrey, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 21 April 1621, in Farley Green, Surrey, England, at the age of 62.

Il a épousé Elizabetha Aldriche le 8 novembre 1590, à Albury, Surrey, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 21 avril 1621, à Farley Green, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard John Deane de Farleyが1559年3月29日にイギリススリーアルブリーで生まれた時，父Richard Farleyは27歳，母Jane Arrowは24歳であった。1590年11月8日にイギリススリーアルブリーでElizabetha Aldricheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1621年4月21日にイギリススリーファルリー グレンで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard John Deane de Farley(이)가 1559년 3월 29일 영국 수리 알버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Farley(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Arrow(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1590년 11월 8일 영국 수리 알버이에서 Elizabetha Aldriche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1621년 4월 21일 영국 수리 패를리 그린에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabetha Aldriche em 8 de novembro de 1590, em Albury, Surrey, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard John Deane de Farley родился 29 марта 1559 г. в местности албури, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Farley было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Arrow, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabetha Aldriche 8 ноября 1590 г. в местности албури, сурри, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 21 апреля 1621 г. в местности фарли грин, сурри, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.
